Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event, such as a workshop, speaker session, or hackathon.

date.description: The date and time of the event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

location.description: The location where the event will be held.

registrationLink.description: A link to register for the event.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents an announcement posted by an administrator.

content.description: The main content of the announcement.

date.description: The date when the announcement was posted.

isPinned.description: Indicates whether the announcement is pinned or not.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CampusConnect platform.

profilePicture.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

registeredEvents.description: References to Events the user is registered for. (Relationship: User 1:N Event)

GalleryItem.title: GalleryItem
GalleryItem.description: Represents an item in the photo gallery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the gallery item.

description.description: A brief description of the image.

definition.description: Stores event details. Anyone can read any event.
Stores announcements posted by admins. Anyone can read any announcement.
Stores user profile information. Only the authenticated user can read/write their own profile.
Stores gallery items (images).  The eventID links the gallery item to a specific event.

params.name: galleryItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the gallery item.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to address the 'permission-denied' error by ensuring clear ownership and access control for each data entity, adhering to the principle of Authorization Independence. Each top-level collection represents a specific entity type, and user-specific data is nested under the `/users/{userId}` path, granting the user full control over their own data.

- **Events:** Stored in a top-level `events` collection.  Any user can read events to support the QAP for displaying a list of events.
- **Announcements:** Stored in a top-level `announcements` collection. Any user can read announcements to support the QAP for listing them.
- **Users:** User profiles are stored in a `users` collection, with individual profiles accessible via `/users/{userId}`.  This path-based ownership ensures that only the authenticated user can modify their profile data.
- **Gallery Items:** Gallery items are stored in a top-level `galleryItems` collection for simplified querying and management. The `eventID` field links each gallery item to a specific event.

This structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules and enables atomic operations.  The segregation of concerns into distinct collections with well-defined access patterns simplifies security rule design and enhances debuggability.  The structure also supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters), ensuring that list operations are secure.
